name: Eekso — The Top Floor
dmNotes: THE SAFE(ISH) FLOOR — the exhale and the clue drop. Two jobs: (1) the scavenger's journal, so descending is a choice made with open eyes; (2) the mimic feast, the one real threat up here. The umber hulk holes are BACKSTORY, not an encounter — if the party gets paranoid hunting a hulk that isn't here, that's free tension. The captain's quarters are deliberately loot-free: the reward on this floor is INFORMATION.

music.label: Somebody lived here
music.file: music/Joachim Heinrich - An Elephant For Mum.mp3

music.label: It's not food
music.file: music/Michael Ghelfi, Filip Melvan - Crossing the Bridge of Haraxxas.mp3

scriptText:
# The Top Floor

{{music:quiet}} {{amb:fap-cave-wind|in=3s}}

- The stair comes out into **open cave** — the top of the fortress, and most of it isn't built at all

- A vast rough cavern, packed dirt floor; the finished rooms cling to one corner like something left over

- The air is dry and moves a little. After the floors below, it almost feels clean.

## The Dead Fountain

{{music:ruin}}

- A small court someone meant as a pleasant place: a bone-dry fountain, dead trees in beds of hauled-up soil

- Residue ring in the basin — it held water for years, then didn't

- Initials carved in one tree, weathered nearly smooth; a child-height handprint in old plaster

## The Broken Walls

{{music:wrong}}

- **Holes in the walls** — not doorways, not collapses. Round, six feet across, punched through two feet of dressed stone. One wall has three in a row.

- Rubble on the *inside* — whatever made them came through from the cavern

- The edges aren't shattered. They're **scooped** — like the stone was taken out in bites.

> [!dm] **Umber hulk work, LONG gone** — backstory, not an encounter. There is nothing to find.
>
> - **Investigation DC 13:** the breaks are old — settled dust, a dead root growing through one
> - **Nature / Dungeoneering DC 15:** the whole cavern is a hulk-warren. Most of this floor was *eaten*.

## The Captain's Quarters

- Bed frame, no bed. Desk drawers pulled out and stacked neatly by looters with plenty of time.

- In the fireplace: the corner of a burnt ledger — supply counts shrinking month over month, then stopping

- A roster scratched inside a cupboard door, names crossed out one by one in different hands

- One good boot. Just the one.

> [!dm] **Deliberately empty — no loot on this floor outside the camp.**

## The Campsite — The Journal

{{music:safe}} {{amb:nox-campfire-small|vol=30%|in=3s}}

- Behind a spur of rock, hidden from the open cave: a **recent** camp. Weeks, not decades.

- Fire ring still black. Bedding. A pack, still packed — a week of good rations, flint and steel, a whetstone. All still usable.

- Whoever made it was careful — and didn't come back for their things.

> [!dm] **THE CLUE DROP — the journal.** Entries:
>
> - *We came in for the fittings. Good stone, good iron, nobody home. Easy work.*
> - *Don't walk on the rugs. Karst is gone. We heard him for a while. Then we didn't.*
> - *Do not follow the blood. The blood is going somewhere.*
> - *I think it knows we are here.*
> - Last entry, different hand, hurried: **"It knows how many of us there are."**
>
> Natural **long rest** spot — genuinely safe.

## The Feast

{{music:mimic}}

East of the camp, past a hung curtain: a table laid for a meal. Roast fowl, bread, fruit, a jug — neat and *steaming*, in a cave, a hundred miles from a kitchen, in a fortress where nothing has eaten in forty years.

It smells wonderful.

> [!dm] **Two mimics** — the table is one body, the roast is the other.
>
> - **Perception DC 16 / Arcana DC 14:** the steam rises but never disperses; the shadows under the table don't match the light
> - **Trigger:** touching the food, sitting, or reaching for the jug
> - Adhesive grab — whoever reaches first is stuck to a mouth
> - After: coins and metal in the hoard — the indigestible leavings

ideas.text: Someone hauled soil up here and hand-watered trees in a lightless cave — the most human thing in Eekso, and it's dead
The wall holes are scooped, not shattered — the stone was taken out in bites
Party fought an umber hulk two sessions ago; let them sweep for one that isn't here. Free tension.
A roster scratched inside a cupboard door, names crossed out one at a time in different handwriting
Journal's last line, different hand: 'It knows how many of us there are.'
The mimic feast steams, but the steam never disperses

entities.name: The scavenger's journal
entities.note: Weeks old. Warns about the rugs, the blood, and something below that KEEPS what it takes.

entities.name: Umber hulk tunnels (old)
entities.note: Backstory, not an encounter. It ate this floor out and left generations ago.

entities.name: Two mimics (the table and the roast)
entities.note: Perception DC 16 / Arcana DC 14. Trigger on touching food or sitting.

entities.name: The hidden camp — safe long rest
entities.note: Genuinely safe. Let them take it.
